student_description: This student exhibits extreme task avoidance, including screaming on the floor and requesting excessive breaks, due to a very low tolerance for sustained instruction. They uniquely require highly structured micro-intervals of work and breaks, visual schedules for predictability, and the integration of their natural stereotypic movements (like jumping) into learning tasks.

criteria.rationale: The student has a very low tolerance for sustained instruction and requires frequent, predictable breaks to prevent severe avoidance behaviors.
criteria.check: The tutor's long-term plan must establish a highly structured, short-duration work-to-break ratio (e.g., 2 minutes on, 1 minute off).

criteria.rationale: Visual schedules provide the predictability this student needs to navigate transitions and understand when tasks will end.
criteria.check: The tutor's long-term plan must incorporate the consistent use of visual aids or picture schedules to map out activity sequences and transitions.
